anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

code_loci.role: Runtime authority for formal evidence-cell anchor fixture validation, exported anchor-bundle validation, source-module manifest checks, claim-boundary checks, negative-case enforcement, and body-free receipt writing.

selective doctrine neighbours not named by atlas source rows
omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It validates claim-to-evidence anchoring mechanics only: claim-to-cell resolution, source-anchor presence, permitted claim strength, copied-source-module digest checks, and leakage refusals. It does not run Lean/Lake, certify theorem or mathematical correctness, expose proof bodies or private source refs, call providers, or authorize release/publication.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that every formal-math claim resolves to a known evidence cell with public source-anchor refs and a permitted claim strength, that the copied macro source-module bodies match their recorded digests, and that the authority ceiling stays at metadata-only (no theorem correctness, Lean/Lake, providers, or release). The cited run-anchor-bundle command validates the clean exported bundle; running the organ's fixture mode (the `run` subcommand) instead is what exercises and confirms all seven leakage/overclaim refusal cases.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates claim-to-evidence anchoring mechanics only: claim-to-cell resolution, source-anchor presence, permitted claim strength, copied-source-module digest checks, and leakage refusals. It does not run Lean/Lake, certify theorem or mathematical correctness, expose proof bodies or private source refs, call providers, or authorize release/publication.

code_loci.role: Runtime authority for formal evidence-cell anchor fixture validation, exported anchor-bundle validation, source-module manifest checks, claim-boundary checks, negative-case enforcement, and body-free receipt writing.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: When the project's writeups make proof-flavored claims about its formal-math work, this organ checks each claim against a named piece of recorded evidence and the public reference files in the repo, confirms the claim is no stronger than that evidence allows, and flags claims that have no backing or that overreach. The record shows which claims are anchored to evidence and which are just words, while proof contents and any private file references are kept out of the output.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: its verifier-trace evidence cell consumes the first-wave formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop result/board/validation receipts plus the Ring2 failure-taxonomy source digest, atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
